title: Westcott neighborhood remains resilient amid recent challenges
summary: The Westcott neighborhood, a mixed-use commercial district known for unique retail and dining, faces challenges from recent break-ins and theater-related incidents that threaten small business viability. City leadership (Mayor Walsh) and neighborhood association (Sam Gruber) emphasize the area's resilience and economic importance while announcing increased police patrols and event enforcement to prevent further deterioration.

political_signal.description: Mayor publicly commits to increased police enforcement and preservation of Westcott as a vibrant mixed-use neighborhood asset, signaling executive prioritization of public safety and economic vitality in this district.

processing_notes: Article discusses recurring crime/vandalism incidents (break-ins fall 2024, recent gunshots) affecting business margins—relevant for police resource allocation analysis
No specific dollar amounts or budget commitments mentioned; qualitative commitment only
Theater-related incidents mentioned but not detailed; may warrant cross-reference with permitting or venue licensing data
Occupancy rate cited (almost 100% retail) is data point for economic-development tracking
